title: Compeerly
description: Title slide — peer-led AI adoption for the enterprise

title: The Gap
description: 40 of 500 stat — the AI adoption gap inside Chico
speakerNotes:
40 out of every 500 licensed users are meaningfully active. Sarah is one of the 460. She has access.
She has the pain point. What she doesn't have is visibility."

title: Why
description: Four customer-pain points we hear on every call
speakerNotes:
i) "The number one thing we hear is that people just don't know where to start. They've got Claude or ChatGPT sitting there and they open it and they don't know what to type. It's not that they don't want to use it — they've never seen someone in their job actually use it."

ii) "And the frustrating part? The people who have figured it out are flying. Saving hours every week. But they're doing it quietly, in their own corner, and nobody else knows. There's no mechanism for that knowledge to travel."

iii) "Middle managers are often the biggest blocker — sceptical, time-poor, and nobody has made the case to them specifically. What if we didn't need them to champion it at all?"

[skip if you're time pressed]
iv) "And the training that companies run to fix this? It's too generic. Nobody learns how to use AI from a company-wide workshop. They learn it from someone who does their exact job showing them exactly what to type."

title: Root Cause
speakerNotes:
"Microsoft Research in 2026 found the root cause. 88% of heavy AI users cited peers as influential in
shaping their AI behaviour. Only 50% of low users did. The gap isn't training. It's visibility. When AI use
stays private, Sarah never sees Marcus." 

[Pause on this slide. Let the stat breathe.]

title: The Bet
speakerNotes:
[Say this slowly. It's your thesis in one sentence]

"AI adoption inside the enterprise won't be top-down. It will be peer-to-peer. Make the shadow workflows
visible."

"If we make private AI use visible and shareable — opt-in, role-filtered, and completely surveillance-free — the people who've never tried AI start seeing exactly how someone in their role uses it. [next slide] That's what changes behaviour."

title: The Product
description: Workflow Feed, Workflow Capture, Nudge Bar
speakerNotes:
Three components, one loop. 
- The Workflow Feed, a library filled with peer submitted AI workflows.
- Secondly an environment where the AI work flow can be captured in around 90 seconds using natural language
- Finally the nut strip, which detects any repetitive pattern mid-session, identifies it and offers a capture form

title: Assumptions
description: Four assumptions plus the riskiest one
speakerNotes:
"The riskiest assumption is that the active 40 will share at all. Before I'd invest in building the capture tool
at scale, I'd run the concierge version first — five conversations, five manually structured workflows,
published to the feed. If non-adopters don't engage with that, the bet is wrong and I need to know that
before I've built anything."

title: The Demo
description: Transition slide — live walk-through of Workflow Feed, Capture, and Nudge Bar
speakerNotes: "That's the thinking. Now let me show you what I actually built."

title: The Metric
description: One metric: activation rate among non-active users exposed to peer workflows
speakerNotes:
"One metric. Activation rate among non-active users exposed to peer workflows."
Activation means 2 or more AI tool uses in a 7-day window after viewing a peer workflow. Target: 15% within 30 days."

title: ROI Model
speakerNotes:
"If we take Chico.ai's own stat — 40 active out of 500 — and apply a conservative 15% activation rate to the non-active pool, that's 69 new active users."

2 hours saved per week at a £50 blended rate — £6,900 per week. Annualised,
per enterprise client: £358,800."

The weakest assumption is the £50 blended rate — it varies by role and industry. The model is
directionally correct but that number should be validated against Chico.ai's actual customer data in the
pilot. I'm presenting this as a model, not a fact."

title: The Pilot
speakerNotes:
"We don't need to build more to validate this. The pilot is five steps: one client, 10 active users interviewed, their workflows manually curated and published, surfaced to 50 non-active users in matched roles, 30 days, measure activation rate. No additional engineering lift beyond a new tab in the existing dashboard."

"The companies that win with AI won't be the ones who bought the best tools. They'll be the ones who made AI genuinely useful for every person — at the role level. Compeerly is how Chico.ai owns that layer."

title: Thank You
description: Closing slide — thank you and Q&A
